claim_control.claim_boundary: This map can rank hypotheses and evidence. It cannot infer hidden nets, safe power, or repair release from images alone. Authority still comes from measured topology, simulation, controlled outcome, and release artifacts.

active_closure.summary: Whether the engine has a concrete route to collapse unknowns.

evidence: No terminal controlled bench outcome proves the target function.

function_hypothesis.summary: Likely board purpose inferred from markings, connectors, capabilities, and topology.

simulation.summary: Deterministic low-voltage simulation or bounded source/load envelope.

visual_observability.summary: Photos and visual evidence are enough to create candidate inventory.

visual_topology.summary: Candidate component/connector/trace topology from layout evidence.

evidence_graph.claim_boundary: This graph shows evidence available inside the board session. Claims without capture, measurement, review, reference, or outcome support remain weak.

evidence.source_policy: Repeated visual observations raise candidate confidence but do not prove pinout, nets, voltage, or safe reuse.

evidence.next_unlock: Attach measured topology_evidence.v1 generated from the visual measurement queue.

evidence.claim_boundary: Economic estimate is heuristic until a successful function outcome is recorded.
evidence.rationale: Visual evidence can identify candidates, but value depends on measured function.
evidence.recommended_exit: Collect baseline measurements before spending parts or time.

known_facts.evidence: Required capabilities are still missing from the selected resource set.
known_facts.label: hardware plan status

next_prompts: Run the Unknown low-voltage board baseline protocol and attach trusted artifacts for: resistance, continuity, voltage, current, thermal, review.
Attach trusted continuity evidence with instrument id, calibration status, operator, timestamp, and artifact URI.
Attach trusted current evidence with instrument id, calibration status, operator, timestamp, and artifact URI.
Attach trusted resistance evidence with instrument id, calibration status, operator, timestamp, and artifact URI.
Attach trusted thermal evidence with instrument id, calibration status, operator, timestamp, and artifact URI.
Attach trusted voltage evidence with instrument id, calibration status, operator, timestamp, and artifact URI.
Create a topology_evidence.v1 record from measured connector pinout, continuity, no-short, voltage, current, and thermal observations.
Map 40-pin GPIO header: pin count, pin-1/orientation, ground, supply pins, signal pins, and no-connect pins by continuity or datasheet.
Capture readable close-ups of every IC marking, connector label, regulator marking, crystal, and silkscreen model text.
Add an overview photo or annotated crop with normalized boxes for unmapped evidence items.
Recapture the main components/connectors from another angle so single-photo claims can be cross-checked.
If accessible, capture the backside, solder side, cable underside, or hidden area so hidden routes and damage are not silently assumed.
Add more board photos from different distances or angles.
Capture a wider photo that includes readable markings and their surrounding component context.

procedure: Use Qwen/vision to identify candidate connectors, rails, labels, and likely pin groups.
Measure ground continuity and power-to-ground no-short.
Measure rail voltage and polarity under current limit.
Submit topology_evidence.v1 or a versioned netlist and rerun the field operator.
